meta.sourceTitle: Department of Defense Official Page
meta.notes: Primary statutory name remains Department of Defense; 'Department of War' was added as a secondary ceremonial title by Executive Order on September 5, 2025.

meta.sourceTitle: Secretary of Defense Official Page
meta.notes: Current holder: Pete Hegseth. Known ceremonially as Secretary of War.

meta.sourceTitle: Deputy Secretary of Defense Official Page
meta.notes: Current holder: Steve Feinberg. Known ceremonially as Deputy Secretary of War.

nodes.label: USD(P)
nodes.fullName: Under Secretary of Defense for Policy

meta.sourceTitle: Under Secretary of Defense for Policy Official Page
meta.notes: Current holder: Elbridge Colby. Formulates national security and defense policy.

nodes.label: USD(R&E)
nodes.fullName: Under Secretary of Defense for Research and Engineering

meta.sourceTitle: Under Secretary of Defense for Research and Engineering Official Page
meta.notes: Current holder: Emil Michael. Serves as Chief Technology Officer.

nodes.label: USD(A&S)
nodes.fullName: Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment

meta.sourceTitle: Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment Official Page
meta.notes: Defense Acquisition Executive. Current office holder: Michael P. Duffey.

nodes.label: USD(C)
nodes.fullName: Under Secretary of Defense (Comptroller) / Chief Financial Officer

meta.sourceTitle: Under Secretary of Defense (Comptroller) / Chief Financial Officer Official Page
meta.notes: Current holder: Jules Hurst III. Serves as Chief Financial Officer.

nodes.label: USD(P&R)
nodes.fullName: Under Secretary of Defense for Personnel and Readiness

meta.sourceTitle: Under Secretary of Defense for Personnel and Readiness Official Page
meta.notes: Manages total force readiness, health, and personnel matters.

nodes.label: USD(I&S)
nodes.fullName: Under Secretary of Defense for Intelligence and Security

meta.sourceTitle: Under Secretary of Defense for Intelligence and Security Official Page
meta.notes: Manages defense intelligence and security enterprises.

nodes.label: CDAO
nodes.fullName: Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Office

meta.sourceTitle: Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Office Official Page
meta.notes: Coordinates enterprise artificial intelligence, data, and analytics.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Innovation Unit Official Page
meta.notes: Accelerates commercial tech transition to the military; directly reports to SecDef.

meta.sourceTitle: Office of Strategic Capital Official Page
meta.notes: Formed to crowd-in private capital to critical defense technologies.

meta.sourceTitle: Strategic Capabilities Office Official Page
meta.notes: Repurposes existing platforms to counter strategic threats; reports to DepSecDef.

nodes.label: CAPE
nodes.fullName: Director of Cost Assessment and Program Evaluation

meta.sourceTitle: Director of Cost Assessment and Program Evaluation Official Page
meta.notes: Principal advisor to SecDef for independent program analysis and cost estimation.

nodes.label: DOT&E
nodes.fullName: Director of Operational Test and Evaluation

meta.sourceTitle: Director of Operational Test and Evaluation Official Page
meta.notes: Reports directly to SecDef on operational test and evaluation matters.

nodes.label: DoD CIO
nodes.fullName: Chief Information Officer of the Department of Defense

meta.sourceTitle: Chief Information Officer of the Department of Defense Official Page
meta.notes: Information technology, cloud, network, and spectrum spectrum advisor.

nodes.label: GC DoD
nodes.fullName: General Counsel of the Department of Defense

meta.sourceTitle: General Counsel of the Department of Defense Official Page
meta.notes: Principal legal officer of the Department.

nodes.label: DoD IG
nodes.fullName: Inspector General of the Department of Defense

meta.sourceTitle: Inspector General of the Department of Defense Official Page
meta.notes: Statutorily independent, but placed within OSD for administrative oversight.

meta.sourceTitle: Director of Administration and Management Official Page
meta.notes: Advises SecDef on administrative, organizational, and security policy matters.

nodes.label: CJCS
nodes.fullName: Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff

meta.sourceTitle: Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Official Page
meta.notes: Principal military advisor to the President and SecDef; strictly advisory. Current holder: Gen. Dan Caine.

nodes.label: VCJCS
nodes.fullName: Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff

meta.sourceTitle: Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Official Page
meta.notes: Assists the CJCS. Current holder: Gen. Christopher J. Mahoney.

meta.sourceTitle: Joint Chiefs of Staff Official Page
meta.notes: Advisory body consisting of CJCS, VCJCS, and Service Chiefs.

meta.sourceTitle: The Joint Staff Official Page
meta.notes: Managed by the Director of the Joint Staff (DJS).

meta.sourceTitle: United States Africa Command Official Page
meta.notes: Geographic CCMD. HQ: Kelley Barracks, Stuttgart, Germany. Commander: Gen. Dagvin R.M. Anderson.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Central Command Official Page
meta.notes: Geographic CCMD. HQ: MacDill AFB, FL. Commander: Admiral Brad Cooper.

meta.sourceTitle: United States European Command Official Page
meta.notes: Geographic CCMD. HQ: Patch Barracks, Stuttgart, Germany. Commander: Gen. Alexus G. Grynkewich.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Indo-Pacific Command Official Page
meta.notes: Geographic CCMD. HQ: Camp H.M. Smith, HI. Commander: Admiral Samuel Paparo.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Northern Command Official Page
meta.notes: Geographic CCMD. HQ: Peterson AFB, CO. Commander: Gen. Gregory M. Guillot.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Southern Command Official Page
meta.notes: Geographic CCMD. HQ: Miami, FL. Commander: Gen. Francis L. Donovan.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Space Command Official Page
meta.notes: Functional CCMD. HQ: Peterson AFB, CO (moving to Huntsville, AL). Commander: Gen. Stephen N. Whiting.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Cyber Command Official Page
meta.notes: Functional CCMD. HQ: Fort Meade, MD. Commander: Gen. Joshua M. Rudd.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Special Operations Command Official Page
meta.notes: Functional CCMD. HQ: MacDill AFB, FL. Commander: Admiral Frank M. Bradley.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Strategic Command Official Page
meta.notes: Functional CCMD. HQ: Offutt AFB, NE. Commander: Admiral Richard A. Correll.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Transportation Command Official Page
meta.notes: Functional CCMD. HQ: Scott AFB, IL. Commander: Gen. Randall Reed.

meta.sourceTitle: Department of the Army Official Page
meta.notes: Secretariat level. Subordinate service is the United States Army.

meta.sourceTitle: Department of the Navy Official Page
meta.notes: Secretariat level. Subordinate services are Navy and Marine Corps.

meta.sourceTitle: Department of the Air Force Official Page
meta.notes: Secretariat level. Subordinate services are Air Force and Space Force.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Army Official Page
meta.notes: Headed by the Chief of Staff of the Army (CSA); current acting holder: Gen. Christopher LaNeve.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Navy Official Page
meta.notes: Headed by the Chief of Naval Operations (CNO); current holder: Admiral Daryl Caudle.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Marine Corps Official Page
meta.notes: Headed by the Commandant of the Marine Corps (CMC); current holder: Gen. Eric Smith.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Air Force Official Page
meta.notes: Headed by the Chief of Staff of the Air Force (CSAF); current holder: Gen. Kenneth S. Wilsbach.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Space Force Official Page
meta.notes: Headed by the Chief of Space Operations (CSO); current holder: Gen. B. Chance Saltzman.

meta.sourceTitle: Director of the Joint Staff Official Page
meta.notes: VADM Frederick W. Kacher leads the Joint Staff.

nodes.label: J1
nodes.fullName: Joint Staff Personnel and Manpower Directorate

meta.sourceTitle: Joint Staff Personnel and Manpower Directorate Official Page
meta.notes: Manages joint workforce and human resources.

meta.sourceTitle: Joint Staff Intelligence Directorate Official Page
meta.notes: Coordinates with DIA; provides intelligence support to JCS.

meta.sourceTitle: Joint Staff Operations Directorate Official Page
meta.notes: Oversees global operations monitoring and planning.

meta.sourceTitle: Joint Staff Logistics Directorate Official Page
meta.notes: Handles strategic and joint logistics policy.

nodes.label: J5
nodes.fullName: Joint Staff Strategic Plans and Policy Directorate

meta.sourceTitle: Joint Staff Strategic Plans and Policy Directorate Official Page
meta.notes: Formulates long-term strategy, plans, and military policies.

nodes.label: J6
nodes.fullName: Joint Staff Command, Control, Communications, and Computers / Cyber Directorate

meta.sourceTitle: Joint Staff Command, Control, Communications, and Computers / Cyber Directorate Official Page
meta.notes: Advises on C4 networks and interoperability.

nodes.label: J7
nodes.fullName: Joint Staff Joint Force Development Directorate

meta.sourceTitle: Joint Staff Joint Force Development Directorate Official Page
meta.notes: Responsible for doctrine, training, and military education.

nodes.label: J8
nodes.fullName: Joint Staff Force Structure, Resources, and Assessment Directorate

meta.sourceTitle: Joint Staff Force Structure, Resources, and Assessment Directorate Official Page
meta.notes: Validates military requirements via JCIDS and JROC.

nodes.label: ASD(SPC)
nodes.fullName: Assistant Secretary of Defense for Strategy, Plans, and Forces

meta.sourceTitle: Assistant Secretary of Defense for Strategy, Plans, and Capabilities Official Page
meta.notes: Develops strategy, force planning, and nuclear posture.

nodes.label: ASD(SO/LIC)
nodes.fullName: Assistant Secretary of Defense for Special Operations and Low-Intensity Conflict

meta.sourceTitle: Assistant Secretary of Defense for Special Operations and Low-Intensity Conflict Official Page
meta.notes: Has statutory administrative oversight of USSOCOM forces.

nodes.label: ASD(HD&HA)
nodes.fullName: Assistant Secretary of Defense for Homeland Defense and Americas Security Affairs

meta.notes: Formulates homeland defense and Western Hemisphere security policies.

nodes.fullName: Assistant Secretary of Defense for Critical Technologies

meta.sourceTitle: United States Army Forces Command Official Page
meta.notes: HQ: Fort Liberty, NC. Prepares and deploys combat-ready land forces.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Army Materiel Command Official Page
meta.notes: HQ: Redstone Arsenal, AL. Provides technology, acquisition support, and logistics.

nodes.label: T2COM
nodes.fullName: United States Army Transformation and Training Command

meta.sourceTitle: United States Army Transformation and Training Command Official Page
meta.notes: HQ: Austin, TX. Consolidated Futures Command and TRADOC in major 2026 restructure. Major 2026 restructure; verify against Army General Orders / official command-structure page before treating as high confidence.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Army Cyber Command Official Page
meta.notes: HQ: Fort Eisenhower, GA. Army component to USCYBERCOM.

nodes.label: USASOC
nodes.fullName: United States Army Special Operations Command

meta.sourceTitle: United States Army Special Operations Command Official Page
meta.notes: HQ: Fort Liberty, NC. Army component of USSOCOM.

nodes.label: USAWHC
nodes.fullName: United States Army Western Hemisphere Command

meta.sourceTitle: United States Army Western Hemisphere Command Official Page
meta.notes: HQ: Fort Liberty, NC. Consolidated theater army role for NORTHCOM and SOUTHCOM. Major 2026 restructure; verify against Army General Orders / official command-structure page before treating as high confidence.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Fleet Forces Command Official Page
meta.notes: Provides ready naval forces; naval component to USNORTHCOM.

meta.sourceTitle: United States Pacific Fleet Official Page
meta.notes: HQ: Pearl Harbor, HI. Naval component to USINDOPACOM.

meta.sourceTitle: Naval Intelligence Activity Official Page
meta.notes: Echelon II command reporting to OPNAV N2N6 to manage intelligence globally. Provenance is Wikipedia; replace with an official source before treating as high confidence.

meta.sourceTitle: Air Combat Command Official Page
meta.notes: Primary provider of combat air forces.

meta.sourceTitle: Air Mobility Command Official Page
meta.notes: Provides airlift, air refueling, and global transport.

meta.sourceTitle: Air Force Global Strike Command Official Page
meta.notes: Sustains the air-based leg of the nuclear triad.

meta.sourceTitle: Air Force Materiel Command Official Page
meta.notes: Wright-Patterson AFB, OH. Manages lifecycle, technology and testing.

meta.sourceTitle: Air Education and Training Command Official Page
meta.notes: Recruits, trains, and educates Airmen.

meta.sourceTitle: Air Force Reserve Command Official Page
meta.notes: Manages Air Force Reserve units and resources.

meta.sourceTitle: Air Force Special Operations Command Official Page
meta.notes: HQ: Hurlburt Field, FL (near Wright, FL). Air Force component to USSOCOM.

meta.sourceTitle: Cyber National Mission Force Official Page
meta.notes: Fort Meade, MD. Subordinate unified command under USCYBERCOM.

nodes.label: JFHQ-DODIN
nodes.fullName: Joint Force Headquarters - Department of Defense Information Network

meta.sourceTitle: Joint Force Headquarters - Department of Defense Information Network Official Page
meta.notes: Fort Meade, MD. Manages cybersecurity operations across the DoD enterprise network.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Arlington, VA. High-risk military research agency.

meta.sourceTitle: Missile Defense Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Huntsville, AL / Pentagon. Oversees layered missile defense development.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Technical Information Center Official Page
meta.notes: Fort Belvoir, VA. Repository for research data.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Test Resource Management Center Official Page
meta.notes: Pentagon. Manages test and evaluation infrastructure.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Logistics Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Fort Belvoir, VA. Handles military logistics and supply chains. Director: LTG Mark T. Simerly.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Contract Management Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Fort Gregg-Adams, VA. Manages contract administrative support.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Threat Reduction Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Fort Belvoir, VA. Counters chemical, biological, and nuclear threats.

nodes.label: OLDCC
nodes.fullName: Office of Local Defense Community Cooperation

meta.sourceTitle: Office of Local Defense Community Cooperation Official Page
meta.notes: Arlington, VA. Manages grant funding to military defense communities.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Contract Audit Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Fort Belvoir, VA. Performs financial audits on defense contracts.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Finance and Accounting Service Official Page
meta.notes: Indianapolis, IN / Columbus, OH. Handles financial and pay systems across DoD.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Health Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Falls Church, VA. Integrates military medical facilities and TRICARE.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Commissary Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Fort Gregg-Adams, VA. Manages commissary supermarkets.

meta.sourceTitle: Department of Defense Education Activity Official Page
meta.notes: Alexandria, VA. Operates primary/secondary schools for dependents. Known ceremonially as War Education Activity.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Security Cooperation Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Pentagon. Manages security assistance and military sales.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense POW/MIA Accounting Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Joint Base Pearl Harbor-Hickam, HI. Conducts recovery and identification missions.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Intelligence Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Joint Base Anacostia-Bolling, DC. Lead defense-wide intelligence agency.

meta.sourceTitle: National Security Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Fort Meade, MD. Lead national and military cryptologic agency.

meta.sourceTitle: National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Springfield, VA. Provides geospatial imagery and analysis.

meta.sourceTitle: National Reconnaissance Office Official Page
meta.notes: Chantilly, VA. Builds and operates U.S. intelligence satellites.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Counterintelligence and Security Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Quantico, VA. Handles background vetting and industrial security.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Information Systems Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Fort Meade, MD. Provides core IT, data networks, and combat communication support.

meta.sourceTitle: Washington Headquarters Services Official Page
meta.notes: Pentagon. Manages OSD administrative services.

meta.sourceTitle: Pentagon Force Protection Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Provides physical protection, law enforcement, and security for the Pentagon.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Legal Services Agency Official Page
meta.notes: Provides legal services and oversight to defense agencies.

meta.sourceTitle: Defense Media Activity Official Page
meta.notes: Primary parent is the Assistant to the SecDef for Public Affairs.

meta.sourceTitle: National Guard Bureau Official Page
meta.notes: Joint activity; Chief of NGB (CNGB) is a member of the JCS. Current: Gen. Steven Nordhaus.

meta.sourceTitle: 1st Special Operations Wing Official Page
meta.notes: Hurlburt Field, FL. Primary Special Ops Wing.

meta.sourceTitle: 24th Special Operations Wing Official Page
meta.notes: Hurlburt Field, FL. Only Special Tactics wing in USAF.

meta.sourceTitle: 492nd Special Operations Wing Official Page
meta.notes: Hurlburt Field, FL. Coordinates special ops training and education.

meta.sourceTitle: 919th Special Operations Wing Official Page
meta.notes: Duke Field, FL. Air Force Reserve Special Operations Wing. Provenance is Wikipedia; replace with an official source before treating as high confidence.

meta.sourceTitle: Air Force Test Center Official Page

meta.notes: HQ: Wright-Patterson AFB, OH. Conducts basic & applied science research.

meta.sourceTitle: 53rd Wing Official Page
meta.notes: Eglin AFB, FL. Operational testing wing.

meta.sourceTitle: 350th Spectrum Warfare Wing Official Page
meta.notes: Eglin AFB, FL. Electromagnetic spectrum warfare operations wing.

meta.sourceTitle: 33rd Fighter Wing Official Page
meta.notes: Eglin AFB, FL. Joint F-35 pilot and maintainer training wing. Provenance is Wikipedia; replace with an official source before treating as high confidence.

meta.sourceTitle: National Military Command Center Official Page
meta.notes: Pentagon war room facility operated under the Joint Staff J-3. Provenance is Wikipedia; replace with an official source before treating as high confidence.

meta.notes: Subordinate center of T2COM focusing on capability and technology development.

meta.sourceTitle: 96th Test Wing Official Page
meta.notes: Eglin AFB, FL (near Wright, FL). Host wing at Eglin AFB.

meta.sourceTitle: Air Force Armament Directorate Official Page
meta.notes: Eglin AFB, FL. PEO weapons acquisition.

edges.notes: AFSOC is the Air Force component of USSOCOM.
USASOC is the Army component of USSOCOM.
ARCYBER is the Army component of USCYBERCOM.
DLA is designated as a Combat Support Agency.
DISA is designated as a Combat Support Agency.
DIA is designated as a Combat Support Agency.
DTRA is designated as a Combat Support Agency.
FORSCOM provides land forces to USNORTHCOM.
USFFC provides naval forces and acts as the naval component to USNORTHCOM.
USPACFLT provides naval forces and acts as the naval component to USINDOPACOM.
919th SOW is in the Air Force Reserve but reports operationally to AFSOC.
The Joint Staff assists and is directed by the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff (10 U.S.C. 155).

Keep the primary administrative parent under USD(P), but add a secondary edge because the OSD chart footnote says the office reports directly to the Secretary for USSOCOM special-operations-peculiar administrative matters. citeturn9view0

meta.title: Department of Defense organizational structure
meta.notes: Includes the ceremonial 'Department of War' context as of Sept 5, 2025, and major 2025/2026 organizational restructuring like Army's T2COM and USAWHC, and Space Force's SPACEFOR-NORTH. Ingested into offline Canvas renderer without independent re-verification.
meta.note: Includes the ceremonial 'Department of War' context as of Sept 5, 2025, and major 2025/2026 organizational restructuring like Army's T2COM and USAWHC, and Space Force's SPACEFOR-NORTH.
